backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor for the SwipeTrack Pro backend that manages attendance records and user profiles with role-based access control, supporting CRUD operations for attendance entries filtered by date range and caller principal.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting the implementation of an Apple-style full-screen calendar overlay for the DailyEntry date picker, listing the affected files and associated test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON map of frontend source file paths to short summaries describing each file's role, serving as a build-planning reference for the frontend codebase.
frontend/index.css: Root Tailwind CSS entry point defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark themes used throughout the application.
frontend/index.html: HTML shell for SwipeTrack Pro with meta tags for SEO, Open Graph, and Apple mobile web app support, referencing the app icon and the Vite/React entry script.
frontend/src/App.tsx: React application root that configures TanStack Router with five page routes, wraps the app in AuthGuard, renders the ProfileSetup modal, and initializes notification reminders.
frontend/src/components/AppleCalendarOve: Full-screen Apple-style calendar overlay component featuring a frosted-glass backdrop, slide-up animation, month navigation, today indicator, selected-date highlight, and swipe-down-to-dismiss gesture.
frontend/src/components/AuthGuard.tsx: Authentication guard component that shows a loading spinner during initialization, redirects unauthenticated users to the LoginScreen, and renders children when authenticated.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: App shell layout component providing a sticky header with logo, sync status, theme toggle, and user dropdown, plus a bottom navigation bar for the five main routes.
frontend/src/components/LoginScreen.tsx: Renders the app's login/welcome screen with a hero section, feature highlights, and an Internet Identity sign-in button that shows a loading spinner while authenticating.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetup.tsx: Displays a modal dialog prompting new users to enter their name for profile setup; only shown when the user profile is fetched and found to be null.
frontend/src/components/SmartSwipeOutPre: Calculates and displays a predicted swipe-out time based on swipe-in time, weekly hours progress, and leave type, or shows a completion message if the weekly target is already met.
frontend/src/components/SyncStatusIndica: Renders a small status indicator icon and label reflecting the current sync state (synced, syncing, offline, or pending with a count).
frontend/src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx: A button component that toggles between light and dark themes using the useTheme hook, displaying a Sun or Moon icon accordingly.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Creates and caches an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) via React Query, reinitializing and invalidating dependent queries whenever the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context with login/logout functions and status flags, persisting identity across page reloads via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useNotificationRemind: Schedules daily browser notifications to remind users to swipe in at 10:00 and swipe out at 18:30 on weekdays, requesting notification permission on mount.
frontend/src/hooks/useOfflineSync.ts: Manages an offline queue of attendance records in localStorage, syncing them to the backend actor when connectivity is restored and exposing sync status and pending count.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Provides React Query hooks for fetching and mutating user profiles and attendance records via the backend actor, including get, save, delete, and date-range queries.
frontend/src/hooks/useTheme.ts: Custom hook that manages light/dark theme state, persists preference to localStorage, and applies the appropriate CSS class to the document root.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports fonts, sets up Tailwind layers, and defines CSS custom properties for light/dark color themes including status, chart, and sidebar colors.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that renders the React app wrapped in QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/Analytics.tsx: Analytics page that displays weekly and monthly bar charts of work hours along with summary stats (average daily/weekly, highest/lowest) derived from attendance records.
frontend/src/pages/DailyEntry.tsx: Daily attendance entry page allowing users to select a date, input swipe-in/out times, breakfast toggle, and leave type, with offline sync support and smart swipe-out prediction.
frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx: Dashboard page showing weekly and monthly attendance summaries including stat cards, progress toward weekly hour targets, and a day-by-day week breakdown.
frontend/src/pages/Export.tsx: Provides a UI page for exporting attendance records (week, month, or all) as CSV or XLSX (TSV fallback) files, building rows with calculated hours and leave labels.
frontend/src/pages/Leaves.tsx: Displays a leave history page showing summary counts of full-day and half-day leaves, with a sorted list of individual leave records fetched from the backend.
frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts: Pure utility module defining constants and functions for parsing times, calculating daily/weekly working hours, applying leave deductions, and formatting time displays.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, persisting, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routing, using sessionStorage for cross-navigation persistence.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the theme with custom OKLCH-based color tokens, font families, border radii, shadows, and enabling typography, container queries, and animate plugins.
project_state.json: Tracks the overall project state (version 11) for a production-ready office attendance tracker React PWA, documenting architectural notes, existing features, known issues, and business rules such as working hour calculations, leave management, and cloud sync.
